summary: Observability is minimal. The TUI application lacks structured logging, distributed tracing, and metrics emission. Error handling is type-safe via Result types but messages are displayed to users rather than logged for operations teams. No correlation IDs, health checks, or metrics instrumentation detected.

sensitive_data_risks: File paths and filenames logged in error messages could leak user directory structure
Plugin load errors shown in UI messages without sanitization

recommendations.title: Implement structured error logging to a file
recommendations.description: Add a simple file-based logger that writes operational events (plugin loads, file operations, errors) in structured format. Consider `tracing` crate which is zero-cost when disabled.

recommendations.title: Add correlation IDs for user sessions
recommendations.description: Generate a session ID on startup and include it in all file operations and error messages. This enables post-hoc analysis of user sessions.

recommendations.title: Implement basic metrics for plugin system
recommendations.description: Track plugin load times, command execution counts, and error frequencies using a simple in-memory metrics store. Export periodically to debug log.

recommendations.title: Sanitize sensitive paths in error messages
recommendations.description: Strip user home directories from error messages shown in the UI to avoid leaking directory structure.
